name: CARTO Spatial Analytics API
what_it_does: Cloud-native spatial analytics platform with REST API for geospatial data analysis, visualization, and enrichment — running spatial SQL directly on cloud data warehouses (BigQuery, Snowflake, Redshift) without moving data.
use_cases: Running spatial SQL analytics on existing BigQuery or Snowflake data without ETL
Creating location intelligence applications with spatial data enrichment APIs
Building site selection and catchment area analysis for retail planning agents
Enriching customer addresses with demographic and POI data from Data Observatory
Generating geospatial visualizations and tilesets for web mapping applications
not_for: Simple geocoding without spatial analytics (use Google Maps or Mapbox)
Real-time GPS tracking without cloud warehouse integration
Small-scale mapping without cloud data warehouse (use Mapbox instead)
best_when: An agent performs large-scale spatial analytics on data already in a cloud data warehouse, needing location intelligence without data movement.
avoid_when: You need simple geocoding or basic map visualization — CARTO is for analytics at scale with cloud warehouse integration.

auth.notes: OAuth2 for user authorization. API key for server-side access to CARTO APIs. Scopes for read, write, and admin operations. Organization-level token management.

pricing.notes: Enterprise-focused pricing. Costs include platform subscription plus cloud warehouse compute costs (charged by BigQuery/Snowflake). No pay-per-request API pricing — subscription model.

performance.rate_limits: API rate limits per organization; analytical queries run against connected cloud warehouse

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Developer documentation covers API v3 endpoints. Spatial SQL documentation extensive. Error responses are JSON with codes and messages. Async queries for large analytical workloads.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Read and analytical queries are idempotent. Tileset creation and data import operations are not — check status before retrying.
known_agent_gotchas: Analytical queries run against cloud warehouse — costs may appear on BigQuery/Snowflake bill
Tileset generation for large datasets can take minutes — async pattern required
CARTO auth token separate from cloud warehouse credentials — two auth systems to manage
Cloud warehouse connection configuration required before any analytical queries

security_score_components.security_notes: SOC2 Type II, ISO 27001, GDPR compliant. OAuth2 for user auth. TLS enforced. Data stays in your cloud warehouse — CARTO doesn't store analytical data. Organization-level access control.
